steps.narrate: The storm arrived without rain. Violet sand climbed into the sky and returned carrying pieces of other lives.
A name struck Tobias's coat and vanished. A childhood room broke against Elia's cheek.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: It is not taking at random. It is testing which memories hold the others in place.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: An excellent observation to survive long enough to publish.

choice.text: Burn the memory of three shadows on the causeway. Turn ordinary companionship into a shield.
Burn the memory of Han. Force the Echo Shell to sing a shelter into the storm.

steps.narrate: Three shadows became blue-white resistance. Arrel held the cape over Elia and Tobias and forgot the morning they had first walked as one group.
Han's remembered hum entered the shell and became a shelter. The old woman's face disappeared from Arrel before the last vibration ended.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: We will write her back into the record if we have to use every wall east of here.

steps.narrate: The storm reached its center. Elia fell first. Tobias followed, both hands locked around the notebook.
Arrel remained standing. The violet pressure bent around him as if it recognized an older rule.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: It cannot classify you. Arrel, that should be impossible.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: For once, I agree that ignorance may be tactically useful.

steps.narrate: The storm passed east. Nothing it touched returned with exactly the same edges.
Elia looked at Arrel's unbroken silhouette and closed the page before Tobias could read her conclusion.
An Authority broadcast crossed the clearing: a Living Funeral would begin at noon. The condemned name was Tobias Vale.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: That is inconvenient. I have not finished the footnotes.
